reviews.review_text:
To me, this is the best CoD game ever made. I used to play the cracked version and probably have around 6,000 hours in it. I highly recommend the campaign, and if you can find servers, the multiplayer is really fun as well.

If you're installing the game for the first time and want to play multiplayer, use the CodX community client — it makes it much easier to find servers.

REVIEWS ARE BASED ON THE TIME I PLAYED

Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare is pure peak gaming — fast, action‑packed, and carried by the absolute GOAT, Captain Price. The campaign still slaps today with iconic missions, clean gunplay, and nonstop intensity that makes it super replayable. But the multiplayer? Yeah, that’s where the fun stops. The old servers are unsafe, people can pull your IP, and it’s just not worth risking anything. Stick to the campaign for a legendary experience and avoid multiplayer at all costs.

no se que tan revolucionario fue pero hay una evolución enorme respecto a los dos primeros juegos, hasta ahora es el primer juego de la saga que disfruté hasta en su máxima dificultad. las misiones son más guiadas y menos permisivas. como en los anteriores juegos, hay mucha prueba y error si jugas en veterano xq el juego no perdona cuando te posicionas mal. acá hay más herramientas, destaco la flashbang xq me parece una utilidad muy canchera para hacerte espacio en un lugar cerrado. también pude jugar el online de hoy, seguramente no es lo que fue en su época pero está muy bien

remember... switching to your pistol is always faster than reloading
Best cod campaign, very fun, i dont mind the 'dated' graphics

I think this game was the culmination of everything shooters had been evolving into before it, and I feel like no military shooter, including other CoDs, has hit like this since. It all just clicked, here, man.
Classic! Works great, just need to run in compatability mode for Win 8 and diable full screen optimisations so that it doesn't crash. Otherwise it works great at top graphics settings.
This was my introduction to the series...i last played it 14 years ago and seeing where the series is heading nowadays its good to remind me about the good ol times

One of my favorite COD games ever. For the time when this game came out, it was honestly peak. The missions were amazing, really well made and iconic, with some of the best characters ever created. Visually and soundwise, the game felt almost like you were actually inside an action movie. The gameplay was simple, clean and super replayable. And I also have to say that COD4 was the first game where I really experienced multiplayer in a bigger way. I spent countless hours on different servers just playing and having fun and it honestly never got bored. Back in the days, the multiplayer community was something special. Even if today most servers are no longer active. The campaign alone is already enough reason to recommend this game. So many legendary moments and missions that people still remember even today. 

Overall, this game is just a classic and will always have a special place for me.

9.6/10
absolute of 10/10  peak of a great franchise , but it would be good if it had achievements to track gameplay

This is the military shooter that redefined the genre. Makes you feel like youre stepping into the shoes of the soldiers youre playing as. The marines sections of cod 4 genuinely feel like youre playing black hawk down the video game. Some of the marine sections with the tight buildings and clearing them feels like the battle of fallujah where nothing is safe and you are vulnerable to any angle your enemy could be watching you from. The SAS feel like they are capable of completing any mission thrown their way which is very reminiscent to how the real life counterpart operates as they truly are in a league of their own. They make us get attatched to the characters we play as because they are built so well and we see how capable they are from mission 1 where we storm the boat. Excellent campaign overall with some of the most iconic set pieces in the shooter genre. Missions like all ghillied up makes you truly feel like youre a top class soldier sneaking into position to take out a high profile target. The immersion this game provides the player is nothing short of astonishing. The gunplay is so clean and a breath of fresh air, tight nit story that never branches into unbelievable territory and set up a lot of great games from the golden age of call of duty. This game is truly 1 of the best games ever made from start to finish the story just reels you in and stays at that intensity til the credits roll. The multiplayer was fantastic too giving us create a class, excellent maps, and great guns to choose from. Even the menu music and soldier silhouette that they used from a real life soldier in the Iraq war which its very clear this call of duty took huge inspiration from shows the amount of passion the devs had while making this game to feel very based in reality and grounded. Overall I think this game could be argued as the best game in the franchise as there isnt a single part that feels lacking in any way. Id have to give this game a 10/10 for how much they put into this and for a game from 2007 it still holds up to this day. After seeing everything this game has to offer you can see why this game truly shook not only military shooters but the entire fps genre as a whole
this game is so buggy and disfunctional its not even fun to play when its not failing because im always waiting for something to go wrong and i have to restart the missions

After playing cod 1 and 2 for the first times ever, it really puts this game into perspective how groundbreaking it was. The presentation, tone, and setting change makes it stand out, but it's understandable why it's not talked about much anymore besides being the blueprint for later games that stand out more today, the level design and set pieces are not as memorable or thrilling as later games, but still a 10/10 playthrough

Entire campaign is less than 6.5 hours. It's high energy, but also feels frantic because you have to listen to orders from the NPC squad leader or fail the mission; and it's not always clear what to do.

Good little nostalgia romp, still an amazing game, and definitely worth it for new comers if they're interested in the history of FPS design and what that era of gaming was like. Would definitely play this again before trying to play one of the modern ones again.

I only got this game just to be able to play the campaign again and I am not disappointed at all.

modern warfare still to this day holds up well and I haven't had any issues with it including gameplay. Didn't run into any bugs or glitches or anything noticeable at least for me.

Glad I was able to get a whiff of nostalgia and the chaos this game has around it, it was fun then and it's fun now.
i dont care if this game looks dated this might have been the most fun Ive had in a shooter campaign ever what great story and level design
can play on basically everything the only downside is i couldnt beat the game on veteran because of one shot one kill but very fun game
Campaign is solid. Mechanics still hold up well. Loved playing the USMC part of the campaign, minus the nuke part...

Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare — The Beginning of a Legendary Era

2007 was an unbelievable year for gaming — so many masterpieces came out, and almost all of them became legendary hits. Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare was one of them, and it was also the game that introduced me to the Call of Duty series.

At the time, it completely changed the feeling of military shooters. The cinematic presentation, intense action, and modern setting felt fresh and revolutionary. And the missions? So many of them became iconic.

The Pripyat missions especially stood out. Sneaking through the abandoned city with Captain Price created an atmosphere that stayed with players for years. Of course, Pripyat here isn’t on the same level as S.T.A.L.K.E.R. when it comes to immersion and post-Soviet atmosphere — but it was still incredibly memorable and tense.

Modern Warfare wasn’t just another FPS. It was the start of a new era for the genre and one of the defining games of the late 2000s.
great game, would recommend to everyone who likes shooters
the most playable fps game ever. i*ve been blatting peeps since 2007 and still come back to it every time.
With MW4 on the horizon, I decided to go back and do a playthrough. the game that put cod on the map, theres even still a live multiplayer community. buy this on sale.
Kinda annoying to constantly use cg_fov everytime a level loads, since I cannot tolerate the low FOV, but as for the game itself, it's pretty good.

9/10

This game is peak 2007 military shooter energy and I genuinely love how unapologetically militaristic it feels. Boots on the ground, special forces ops, helicopters, AC-130 support, iconic one-liners  this was the era where military FPS games had an aura.

And ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ the AC-130 mission (*Death From Above*) was absolute cinema. Getting to control the C-130/Angel of Death gunship with all its different cannons and just raining destruction from above was probably my favorite mission in the whole game. I don’t care how many years pass, that mission still hits.

Also, revisiting maps I already knew from multiplayer and suddenly seeing them in the campaign was such a cool feeling. It's like your brain goes: *"WAIT... I KNOW THIS PLACE."*

But let’s talk about the absolute GOAT section: the Pripyat missions.
The camouflage mission with Captain MacMillan? Pure peak gaming. The whole infiltration phase sneaking through abandoned Pripyat, avoiding patrols, staying hidden in the grass while enemies walk inches away from you...

🤩 Absolute legendary status.

You can feel the tension even today. No Michael Bay explosion spam needed just atmosphere and perfect pacing.

Only real issue I noticed: if you're playing on modern hardware, LIMIT YOUR FPS. Seriously. I noticed the higher my FPS went, the more the game started acting weird and buggy. Old game engine moment. One second you're a SAS operator, next second physics start fighting for their lives.

Overall:
militaristic as hell, iconic missions, legendary atmosphere, and somehow still insanely fun almost 20 years later.

*"50,000 people used to live here..."*
Yeah... absolute classic.

One of the all time greats in the FPS genre. Although multiplayer is basically dead it's worth playing just for the immersive action packed campaign. Although it's an older game it still holds up quite well and feels good to play.

I remember this game was the game that got me into online multiplayer. I still play this along with World at War and still have a fantastic time! The story is still one of the best in the series filled with memorable moments and with the arcade mode has tons of replay-ability. I wish they'd bring it back in later titles. This multiplayer defined the franchise. Introducing the progression system with camos rewarded to you by completing weapon expert challenges. Ranks that reward you with new weapons and equipment. Perks and custom classes to make builds to fit your playstyle. Mod support keeps the game fresh. Server browser with mini communities still keeping this game alive is a testament to how amazing Call of Duty 4 was and still is. Nearly 2 decades later and Call of Duty 4 holds up as an all time classic. My only complaint is the lack of prestige system in this version of the game. Overall still an all time amazing game and cannot recommend enough!
it is a great game in my opinion but i would recommend buying it on a sale though
fun, but multiplayer is unplayable without the COD4X mod
GOAT CoD game. redownloaded it and it was repopulated thanks to steam sale

good game, even for integrated graphics (i have) the campaign is very fun and easy, it can be changed in settings, very fun shooting with accurate guns and recoil. excellent characters and plot, but those darn hound dogs get on my nerves, also my favourite mission is all ghillied up.
Very good game realistic combat would recommend to someone who likes Call of duty
Classic, although the price is high for an old and outdated game by today's standards

This is a Review Bomb, Call of Duty has become the definition of laziness wrapped in a $70 price tag. Every year it’s the same recycled garbage with the same bugs, the same broken servers, the same hackers, and the same promises that never get fixed. Instead of improving the game, they just pump out overpriced skins and bundles like that’s supposed to distract people from the disaster underneath.

How are decade-old issues STILL a thing? Terrible matchmaking, crashes, packet burst, cheating everywhere, audio problems, broken hit registration, and updates that somehow make the game worse every single time. It honestly feels like the developers don’t even play their own game.

The funniest part is that older COD titles still cost ridiculous prices, as if these games aged like masterpieces. They didn’t. Most of them are abandoned, unsafe on PC, full of hackers, and still sold at premium prices. Imagine charging full price for games you barely maintain.

Call of Duty stopped caring about quality a long time ago. Now it’s just a copy-paste cash machine milking nostalgia while delivering unfinished products year after year. People aren’t angry because they “hate COD.” They’re angry because this franchise used to be great, and now it’s managed by greed and incompetence.

if your father didn't play this game around you when you were 7-8 years old, did you really even have a father?
(this game is fun as hell though in all seriousness, some of the missions are kind of irritating at points but god is it nostalgic regardless. thanks baba for putting me on 👍)

"On Your Feet, Soldier. WE ARE LEAVING"

Classic. An Absolute Classic. 

It's Crazy To Say This But I Had Never Played This Until Now And Now That I Have, I Can Say It Was So Worth It And So Fun. 
The Story Is Still Iconic, The Gameplay Especially On Hard Mode Is Extremely Fun ,Albeit Frustrating At Times But Still Satisfying.

I Love It. 9/10

It is not bad for how old it is. You can still get multiplayer running with third party addon. The only thing I don't like after all those years is the AI in single player. It makes you feel like you are part of a squad but your mates don't cover you at all, you have to do everything all alone and their sole purpose is to block your view all the time. :) That and the fact I had to limit the FPS in Nvidia control panel otherwise I was not able to kill dogs or climb ladders. Few hundred FPS modern PCs can easily get on this game is just too much for the engine. But that's acceptable. Many older games have the same issue (Assassin's Creed 1 and 2 for example).
Found it easy to set up your keybinds and the game play was good.
the pacing is kinda mid but like it or not its a pivotal game in the franchise taking it out of the wwii era
absolute blast from the past back when cod games had personality

Never played Modern Warfare during its prime, but the campaign is solid and cinematic. 

That said, some sections can be really frustrating the way enemies respawn in certain areas, combined with frequent grenades and highly accurate AI which would have been fine if not for the intense screen shakes when hit, can really test your patience

Very poor for activision to release this garbage, and make us pay for this poor performance.
